blocks.narrative: Comprehensive and fully documented block library. Text: text, statement, quote, list. Media: image (multiple layout styles), gallery, audio, video upload/embed, attachment, iframe embed. Interactive: accordion, tabs, labeled graphic (hotspot image), process, timeline, scenario (branching with characters), sorting activity (drag-drop), flashcards, buttons. Assessment: multiple choice, multiple response, fill-in-the-blank, matching (both knowledge check and quiz modes). Chart: bar, line, pie (with hover tooltips). Structural: continue/gate block, divider, spacer. Plus: table, math equation editor, code block (custom HTML/CSS/JS), Storyline 360 embed. AI-generated timeline and table blocks. Block templates saveable and shareable across Teams. Every benchmark block type is confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

assessment.narrative: Full-featured assessment system. Question types confirmed: multiple choice, multiple response, fill-in-the-blank, matching. Knowledge check blocks (unscored, in-lesson) and quiz lessons (scored, graded) are distinct. Question banks confirmed: create named banks, reuse across courses, draw random subsets. Randomisation confirmed: random question order and random answer order both supported. Anchor questions (always-static questions in random quizzes) confirmed. Passing score configurable. Multiple attempts not explicitly confirmed in sources but quiz retry is standard behaviour. No hotspot image question type or drag-drop question type found natively — sorting activity block exists but is not a quiz question type. True/false is achieved via multiple choice. AI quiz generation confirmed (3–25 questions, topic + difficulty control). Evidence: [marketing|docs]

ai.narrative: Articulate AI in Rise 360 is one of the most comprehensive AI feature sets in the market (as of 2025). AI course generation: full course draft from prompt or source document upload (audio, video, Storyline files), with follow-up questions on audience, tone, objectives. AI content rewrite: tone change, grammar check, readability improvement, text expansion. AI quiz generation: 3–25 questions, multiple types, configurable topic and difficulty. AI translation: not explicitly confirmed in Rise (Elucidat has this; Rise's translation story was not surfaced in research — marked unknown). AI image generation: confirmed, with style options (editorial, 3D, cartoon, watercolor), from 13M+ stock library or AI-generated. AI voice/narration: thousands of AI voices filterable by age/gender/use case (Q2 2025). AI captions for video: AI-generated, synced captions (Q4 2025). AI audio transcripts: auto-generated for any audio. AI timeline and table block generation confirmed. AI Writer references surrounding lesson material for context-aware generation. AI features are on a separate paid tier (Articulate 360 AI, $1,449–$1,749/user/year) after a 30-day trial. AI learner feedback and AI tutor/chat not confirmed. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

compliance.narrative: Best-in-class compliance output. SCORM 1.2: confirmed. SCORM 2004: confirmed. xAPI/Tin Can: confirmed. cmi5: confirmed. AICC: confirmed. PDF export: confirmed (noted as not accessible — PDF output lacks accessibility). Video export: not confirmed. LTI consumer/provider: not natively supported — achieved via SCORM upload into an LTI-enabled LMS. Rise supports five LMS interoperability standards from a single built-in Publish flow with no third-party tools. This is the most comprehensive compliance output confirmed in this benchmark. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

analytics.narrative: Analytics story is split between two contexts. In Quick Share (no-LMS delivery): basic light analytics built into Articulate 360 — completion tracking, view data. In Reach 360 (built-in LMS): learner progress, completion tracking, quiz scores, manual completion overrides (Q2 2025), group-level reporting. Analytics are functional and integrated, but Reach 360 is deliberately lightweight — reviewers and analysts describe it as suited for smaller organisations, not enterprise complexity. Time-on-task and cohort analytics are not explicitly confirmed. Report export not confirmed in Reach 360 sources. For organisations using third-party LMSes, analytics depend entirely on the LMS's own reporting capabilities (Rise sends SCORM/xAPI data). Google Analytics integration is community-discussed but not confirmed as a native feature. Scored 3: solid for basic tracking, insufficient for complex enterprise reporting needs. Evidence: [marketing|docs|review]

collaboration.narrative: Collaboration is a confirmed strength of the Articulate 360 ecosystem. Real-time collaborative editing in Rise 360: multiple authors can edit different lessons simultaneously (Teams tier). Course collaborator and course manager roles confirmed. Review 360 is a dedicated stakeholder review platform: inline comments, file attachments on comments, review request tracking with due dates and email notifications, comment subscriptions, bulk email invitation, completion buttons, status tracking, nested folder organisation. Shared team folders in Review 360 with permissions assignment. Multiple authors can publish new versions to the same Review 360 project. Shared block templates across Teams accounts. Question bank sharing across Teams. Roles confirmed: Primary Admin, Admin, Manager, Reporter, Creator, Learner. Version history is tracked per course in Rise 360. Evidence: [marketing|docs|review]

templates.narrative: 1,000+ course templates confirmed, including 100+ for onboarding, plus compliance, HR, DEI, soft skills, information security. Templates are accessible immediately on signup/trial with no friction. Block templates: authors can save and share blocks (with their content) as reusable templates across a Teams account. Three built-in visual themes (Rise, Apex, Horizon) with extensive sub-customisation (cover page layouts, navigation, lesson headers, colors, fonts, progress indicators). Custom font upload and custom hex colors supported. Theme-level white-label feel is achievable (logo, colors, fonts) though a single accent color limits complex palette matching. Custom domain: not confirmed for Rise/Reach 360 in sources. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

branching.narrative: Branching in Rise 360 is available but constrained to specific block types. Scenario blocks support branching roleplay (choice-consequence with characters) — fully confirmed. Continue blocks gate content progression. Personalised learning paths at the course level: not confirmed as a native feature in Rise (Storyline 360 has this via variables; Rise does not expose conditional navigation between lessons natively). Prerequisites between lessons/modules: not confirmed. The branching story is limited to the Scenario block interaction type — sufficient for roleplay scenarios but not for full adaptive learning paths within Rise itself. Embedding Storyline 360 (which has full branching) is the workaround. Scored 3. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

media.narrative: Comprehensive media capabilities. Direct video upload: confirmed. CDN hosting: confirmed (Articulate 360 storage, 150 GB individual / unlimited Teams). Stock media library: Content Library 360 — 13M+ images, photos, illustrations, characters, and video clips, integrated directly into the editor. In-product image editing: not explicitly confirmed (crop/basic adjustments likely but not named as a feature in sources). AI image generation: confirmed (editorial, 3D, cartoon, watercolor styles). Subtitle/caption support: confirmed — AI-generated captions for video (Q4 2025), author-uploaded captions also supported. Audio transcripts: AI-generated or author-written. AI voice/text-to-speech: thousands of voices (Q2 2025). Screen recording: not confirmed natively in Rise (available in Storyline 360 / Replay 360 which are separate Articulate 360 apps). Evidence: [marketing|docs]

accessibility.narrative: Rise 360 officially claims WCAG 2.1 Level AA conformance and publishes a VPAT. Section 508 compliance confirmed. EN 301 549 compliance confirmed. Keyboard navigation: confirmed, with fully visible focus indicators. Screen reader support: confirmed for NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver, and Microsoft Narrator (added Q2 2025). Alt text support: confirmed (author responsibility for content images). Auto color contrast (4.5:1 ratio): confirmed as a theme option. AI-generated audio transcripts and video captions support deaf/HoH learners. Block entrance animations are automatically disabled when learners enable reduced motion. Known gaps (tracked in Articulate's public Accessibility Maturity Plan): keyboard-only users cannot access the nav menu without tabbing through the entire course; Process block lacks visible focus indicator; screen reader language switching per block is unsupported; PDF export is not accessible; video blocks cannot include audio descriptions. Scored 4: strong compliance claim with a published maturity plan, but real-world gaps are documented and known. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

publishing.narrative: Best-in-class publishing. Shareable link (no LMS): confirmed via Quick Share with built-in light analytics. Password protection: not explicitly confirmed in sources for Quick Share. SSO learner access: confirmed via Reach 360 (with Okta OIDC integration); enterprise SAML available. Embed in external site: not explicitly confirmed as a named publishing option (though SCORM in an LMS and iframe embed blocks allow content embedding in practice). Reach 360 direct publish: no zip export required, instant republish without re-upload. SCORM/xAPI/cmi5/AICC export for any LMS. PDF and web publish also available. Evidence: [marketing|docs]

extensibility.narrative: REST API: confirmed for Reach 360 (the delivery/LMS layer) — course, user, enrollment management. Webhooks: confirmed for Reach 360 (course.completed, user.created, enrollment events, with shared secret signing). Zapier/Make: no native connector confirmed — community has raised this. MCP server: none. SSO for authors: confirmed via Okta (OIDC/SWA); enterprise SAML SSO available via enterprise contact. Rise 360 authoring layer has no native REST API — a long-standing community-raised gap with no public roadmap commitment. The extensibility story is strong at the LMS/delivery layer (Reach 360 API) and weak at the authoring/content-creation layer. Evidence: [docs]
